Tests are in test/ directory following project convention

Application layer hooks (Clean Architecture)
useDataGeneration: Manages data generation config and state
useStats: SWR-based stats fetching with validation
Comprehensive test coverage: 6 test files covering bounds, config, events, generation, integration
Depends on domain layer entities and lib layer validation

Factory pattern for repository creation based on environment

Database timeout resilience using withDatabaseTimeout wrapper
Comprehensive test coverage: 13 test files covering repositories and services

Presentation layer hook for UI-specific stats formatting
Wraps application layer useStats with presentation logic
Transforms error messages for display (Japanese localization)
Adds error logging for refetch failures
Clean Architecture compliance: depends only on application layer
No dedicated tests (likely tested through component integration tests)
Presentation layer React components organized in 5 subdirectories: charts, dashboard, ui, common, root
charts/: 8 chart components with comprehensive test coverage (19 test files including edge cases, integration, accessibility)
dashboard/: 3 components (DataVisualization, DataSection, InstructionsSection) with 4 test files
Dashboard.tsx: Main page component with 6 test files (accessibility, dataFetching, interactions, notification, performance, rendering)
ui/: Button component (index.tsx only)
common/: 8 reusable components (ErrorBoundary, LoadingSpinner, ComponentCard, Icons, Notification, PageBreadcrumb, UsageInstructions, GridShape)
Clean Architecture compliance: Presentation layer can import from all layers
Total test coverage: 34 test files covering rendering, logic, edge cases, accessibility, error handling, integration
Component follows INV-ARCH-001: Dashboard split into 6 focused test files
Cross-cutting library layer with 5 subdirectories: resilience, api, config, constants
error-handler.ts: Centralized error handling with custom error classes (AppError, ValidationError, NetworkError)
resilience/: Production-ready resilience patterns
- CircuitBreaker: State machine (CLOSED/OPEN/HALF_OPEN) with configurable thresholds
- StructuredLogger: Visibility-based logging (PUBLIC/INTERNAL/DEBUG/TRACE)
- LoopDetector: Infinite loop detection with iteration limits
- MemoryMonitor: Memory usage tracking and alerts
- timeout.ts: Operation timeout wrappers (api, database, file, e2e)
api/: Next.js API utilities
- response.ts: Typed API response builders
- validation.ts: Zod-based data validation (validateData, validateDataSafe)
- error-handler.ts: API-specific error handling with resilience wrappers (withErrorHandler, withResilientHandler)
config/: Environment configuration with validation (env.ts, index.ts)
constants/: Application constants (API, messages, UI)
prisma.ts: Prisma client singleton with HMR support
Comprehensive test coverage: 24 test files covering all modules
Clean Architecture compliance: Shared library layer, no dependencies on src/ layers

key_exports: Mirage mock server integration in development
notes: Next.js App Router structure with route groups: (chart), (dashboard)
API routes (3 endpoints):
- /api/stats: GET endpoint for statistics with test coverage
- /api/seed: GET/POST endpoints for seed data management with comprehensive tests for both Prisma and Mirage
- /api/health: Health check endpoint with test coverage
Dashboard components co-located in src/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/components/:
- StatsDisplay.tsx (5 test files: data, behavior, styling, states, setup)
- EventManager.tsx (5 test files: adding, interaction, memoization, rendering, removing)
- GenerationControls.tsx (4 test files: edge-cases, interactions, rendering, setup)
- DataGenerationPanel.tsx with test coverage
- ClassCharacteristicsEditor.tsx with test coverage
Page-level tests: dashboard/page.test.tsx
Root layout.tsx: Initializes Mirage mock server in development when NEXT_PUBLIC_MOCK=true
Clean Architecture compliance: Presentation layer (src/app) can import from all layers
Test organization: 21 test files co-located with components following Next.js App Router convention
